creator: bobf@mrp3.com
text: the serial port I/O pin assignment 'confusion' seems to only affect reboot, but not shutdown.  so if I do a shutdown, I see all of the kernel messages and the 'press any key to boot' message.  If I do a reboot, only part of the messages show up.  So it appears that the GPIO pins associated with the serial port may be getting 'reset' differently for poweroff vs reboot.  This might be a hint as to where in the code this is happening, a particular event for a particular driver (for example).  I'm mentioning this in case someone already knows the answer.  It's one of those kinds of indications that might point directly at the problem, if I were more familiar with the internal workings.
